channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Udine on high alert ahead of Italy's match against Israel
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The city of Udine is on high alert as Italy prepares to host Israel in a competitive soccer match on Monday.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli strike on hospital tent camp kills 4 and ignites a fire that burns dozens
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a hospital courtyard in the Gaza Strip early Monday killed at least four people and triggered a fire that swept through a tent camp for people displaced by the war, leaving more than two dozen with severe burns, according to Palestinian medics
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ailing and silenced in prison, Belarus activist symbolizes the nation's repression
item.description: The last time any of Maria Kolesnikova’s family had contact with the imprisoned Belarusian opposition activist was more than 18 months ago.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: South Korea has detected signs North Korea is preparing to destroy parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as tensions soar between them over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris urges Israel to ramp up Gaza aid, citing UN food report
item.description: Vice President Harris on Sunday pushed for Israel to ramp up aid to Gaza, citing a World Food Programme (WFP) report. “The UN reports that no food has entered northern Gaza in nearly 2 weeks.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: China holds large military exercises surrounding Taiwan to warn against independence
item.description: China is holding large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan, deploying an aircraft carrier along with warplanes, underscoring the tense situation in the Taiwan Strait.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Macao's former top judge is chosen as the Chinese casino hub's first leader born in mainland China
item.description: MACAO (AP) — Macao’s former top judge, Sam Hou Fai, was chosen as the Chinese casino hub’ s next leader in a largely ceremonial election on Sunday, setting him up to become the city’s first chief executive born in mainland China.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The U.N. peacekeeping mission in southern Lebanon said Israeli tanks “forcibly entered” one of its positions on Sunday, as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu demanded it leave the area. International criticism is growing after Israeli forces have repeatedly fired on U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s human rights envoy urges response to alleged killings of Ukrainian POWs in Kursk
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s human rights ombudsman urged international organizations Sunday to respond to a claim that several Ukrainian prisoners of war were executed in Russia’s Kursk region, where Kyiv had launched an incursion in August.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops continue a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hindus in Bangladesh celebrate their largest festival under tight security following attacks
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Schoolteacher Supriya Sarker is glad to celebrate Bangladesh's largest Hindu festival of Durga Puja but feels the festivities would be more jubilant without the fear and violence that overshadow this year's event.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM and Al EMRUN GARJON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris faces new battles with expanding Middle East conflict
item.description: Vice President Harris is struggling to confront a rapidly worsening crisis in the Middle East as she seeks to balance unwavering support for Israel with appeals to progressive and Arab American communities.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: The air raid on central Beirut — the deadliest in over a year of war — apparently targeted two residential buildings in separate neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: BILAL HUSSEIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu speak ahead of expected retaliation against Iran
item.description: President Biden spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday for the first time in weeks, amid heightened tensions between the leaders as Israel calibrates its expected retaliatory strike against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
